name: Empanadillas with Cuban Picadillo
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Beef chuck, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
1 large Green Bell pepper, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cup pimiento-stuffed green olives, chopped
2. 1 Egg, beaten
3. 1 tablespoon Milk, cream or water
Prepare pastry: Combine flour and salt. Cut in lard (or shortening). Mix water with lemon juice (or vinegar) and yolks. Add to flour and form into a ball. Chill for 30 minutes. Roll into large thin rectangles, cut into squares, and chill until filling is ready.
3. Prepare filling: Heat oil in a skillet and brown beef cubes. Add salt, pepper, and cayenne, then remove from pan. Sauté onions, garlic, peppers, and chile until soft. Return meat to pan with juices. Add tomatoes, raisins, and vinegar. Cook until meat is very tender (30-45 minutes). Stir in olives and cook for another 10 minutes. Cool to room temperature.
4. Assemble: Place filling onto pastry squares. Fold over and seal. Trim excess pastry while maintaining the triangle shape. Mix egg and milk/cream/water for an egg wash. Brush onto turnovers. Place on greased cookie sheets.
5. Bake in a preheated 375°F oven for 15 minutes until golden. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Makes approximately 4 pastries.
